name: Sør-Trøndelag (Norway, 1983–2017)
description: A gold field charged with two red Saint Olav axes crossed in saltire.
history: Granted to Sor-Trondelag county on 17 June 1983. The county was abolished on 1 January 2018 when it was merged with Nord-Trondelag into the (re-united) Trondelag county.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: red Saint Olav axes on a gold field, alluding to King and Saint Olav Haraldsson, who fell at the Battle of Stiklestad and is closely associated with the Trondelag region
two crossed axes after the attribute of Saint Olav, patron of Norway and especially of Trondelag
